title: Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
gist: A mid-2026 launch-industry roundup: SpaceX's Falcon fleet demonstrates unmatched reusability (1,000th Merlin engine, 6,000 engine flights), legacy vehicles like Pegasus and Atlas V retire, new entrants Skyroot (India) and Isar (Germany) near maiden orbital flights, and Blue Origin races to recover from a New Glenn pad explosion.

insights.claim: SpaceX manufactured its 1,000th Merlin 1D engine, yet because the engine is reusable that fleet has already logged more than 6,000 engine flights.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: A 6-to-1 flights-per-engine ratio quantifies the capex advantage rivals must match; refurbishment beats production.
insights.quote: “With Falcon’s reusability, recovering these engines has enabled continued reliability enhancements, making Merlin one of the most reliable rocket engines ever manufactured,”

insights.claim: The Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy have flown 671 times, mostly on reused boosters and engines, with nine Merlins per Falcon 9 and 27 per Falcon Heavy.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Establishes the scale incumbents and new entrants are competing against on cost-per-launch.

insights.claim: By contrast, Russia's single-use RD-107/108 engines have required more than 10,000 units produced at a Samara plant to power over 2,000 Soyuz and R-7 family flights since the 1950s.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Direct engines-per-flight comparison crystallizes the structural cost gap between expendable and reusable architectures.

insights.claim: India's Skyroot Aerospace will attempt the first test flight of its Vikram-1 rocket between July 12 and August 4, marking India's first private bid to place a vehicle into orbit.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: First private Indian orbital attempt is a milestone for a low-cost launch geography investors are watching.
insights.locator: Launch window set for India’s first commercial rocket

insights.claim: Skyroot has raised roughly $160 million to date, including a $60 million round in May that pushed its valuation over $1 billion, for a rocket designed to lift nearly half a ton to low-Earth orbit.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: A unicorn valuation ahead of first flight prices in binary launch-success risk for private investors.

insights.claim: Germany's Isar Aerospace signed Planet Labs to fly a US-designed, Berlin-assembled Pelican Earth-observation satellite on its still-unproven Spectrum rocket, whose first launch failed in March 2025.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: A commercial anchor customer for an unproven European rocket signals sovereign-launch demand despite failure risk.
insights.quote: “With both satellite and rocket being built in Germany, this launch will be a national first for the country, demonstrating rapid advancements in the nation’s sovereign space capabilities,”

insights.claim: Isar scrubbed its most recent Spectrum launch attempt on June 15 after detecting off-nominal behavior in the vehicle's fluid systems, and has missed several windows since January.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Repeated scrubs quantify schedule risk for backers of Europe's leading private launch startup.

insights.claim: The Katalyst-built Link servicing satellite reached orbit on the final flight of Northrop Grumman's air-launched Pegasus XL, whose usage collapsed as cheaper options like SpaceX and Rocket Lab rose.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Pegasus retirement marks how commercial small-launch pricing has fully displaced legacy air-launch.

insights.claim: United Launch Alliance flew its final Atlas V with a payload fairing and its last five-booster configuration, sending 29 Amazon Leo satellites up on the ninth Atlas V for that constellation.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Atlas V's wind-down leaves ULA dependent on the troubled Vulcan for its competitive future.
insights.locator: End of an era for Atlas V

insights.claim: ULA's next flight will be the return to flight of its Vulcan rocket, which has been grounded since February due to problems with its solid-fueled boosters, while six remaining Atlas Vs are reserved for Boeing Starliner crew flights.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Vulcan's grounding is a live constraint on ULA's ability to compete for national-security launch backlog.

themes.theme: In-orbit servicing and satellite life extension

insights.claim: Katalyst Space Technologies' Link satellite will spend weeks approaching NASA's Swift observatory to raise its altitude, a satellite launched in 2004 that was never designed to be serviced in orbit.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Commercial life-extension of non-serviceable legacy assets validates an emerging in-orbit servicing market.

insights.claim: SpaceX launched the roughly 7-metric-ton SXM-11 satellite for SiriusXM to geostationary orbit, only its third GEO-bound payload out of 78 Falcon launches this year as demand shifts to low-Earth megaconstellations.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: GEO comprising 3 of 78 launches quantifies the structural migration of satcom capital from GEO to LEO.

insights.claim: The SXM-11 manufacturer Lanteris Space Systems, formerly branded Maxar, was acquired by Texas-based Intuitive Machines in January 2026 for about $800 million.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: The ~$800M Maxar/Intuitive Machines deal marks ongoing consolidation among space manufacturers investors can track.

insights.claim: Blue Origin's May 28 New Glenn explosion destroyed the company's only launch pad, including its lightning tower and massive transporter-erector, with early analysis pointing to the aft section of the first stage.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Loss of the sole pad is a hard capacity constraint on Blue Origin's NASA lunar-lander commitments.
insights.locator: NASA pleased with Blue Origin’s pad cleanup

insights.claim: Blue Origin plans to return New Glenn to flight before year-end using a crane to raise the rocket rather than rebuilding the destroyed transporter-erector, a changed concept of operations.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: An aggressive same-year recovery timeline is the key milestone gating NASA's Mk.1 and Mk.2 lunar landers.

insights.claim: NASA selected Rocket Lab to fly two science missions on three Electron rockets in 2027, including the twin-satellite PolSIR ice-cloud mission and the TSIS-2 solar-irradiance sensor.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: Repeat NASA science awards cement Rocket Lab as the default dedicated small-sat launcher.
insights.locator: NASA taps Rocket Lab for three launches

insights.claim: NASA delivered L3Harris-built (formerly Aerojet Rocketdyne) throttling MR-80 hydrazine braking engines for ESA's Rosalind Franklin Mars rover, now set to launch in late 2028 after delays driven mostly by geopolitical tensions.
insights.speaker: Stephen Clark
insights.so_what: US hardware keeping ESA's Mars rover alive shows how geopolitics, not tech, now paces flagship missions.
insights.locator: Rocket engines delivered for Rosalind Franklin
